item.text: I’ll play exactly `turn-005` for P1. The line is to stack Blast plus Zap for a strong damage turn, then use the board phase to kill the exposed raider, reclaim contested supply, and recruit if income makes it legal.

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "printf '%s\\n' '{\"schemaVersion\":1,\"turnId\":\"turn-005\",\"player\":\"P1\",\"actions\":[{\"type\":\"playAction\",\"handIndex\":3},{\"type\":\"playAction\",\"handIndex\":3},{\"type\":\"moveToBuy\"},{\"type\":\"buyCard\",\"cardId\":\"storm\"},{\"type\":\"endTurn\"}]}' > ../runs/codex-rush-vs-engine-01/actions/turn-005.deck.json
bun run --silent cli -- deck-turn --config game/deck.yaml --state ../runs/codex-rush-vs-engine-01/deck.json --actions ../runs/codex-rush-vs-engine-01/actions/turn-005.deck.json --result ../runs/codex-rush-vs-engine-01/results/turn-005.deck.result.json
jq . ../runs/codex-rush-vs-engine-01/results/turn-005.deck.result.json"

item.text: The deck command is taking a moment, so I’m waiting on the CLI result before choosing final board actions. If the 5-cost buy is rejected, I’ll adjust to the best legal 4-cost buy.

Deck produced 3 damage and enough money for `storm`, which fits the rush payload plan. For board play, I’m flipping three more centers, preserving the wounded scout by moving it off the front line, killing P2’s raider with two boosted attacks, and spending the now-legal supply recruit.
The board CLI accepted the full plan: P2’s active raider is removed, P1 recruits a new raider, and P1 now has much stronger center control. I’m committing and running strict validation.

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "bun run --silent playtest -- commit-turn --run ../runs/codex-rush-vs-engine-01 --deck-result ../runs/codex-rush-vs-engine-01/results/turn-005.deck.result.json --board-result ../runs/codex-rush-vs-engine-01/results/turn-005.board.result.json --summary \"P1 played Blast plus Zap, bought Storm, killed P2's forward raider, flipped more centers, and recruited a raider.\" --reasoning \"Played Blast first because it preserved an action, then played Zap for an additional damage counter and enough economy to buy Storm as a high-impact rush payload. On the board, the wounded scout moved from 7,5 to 8,6 to capture center-east while avoiding being the primary blocker. P1-raider-start-1 moved to 7,5 to maintain center-center-south control and set up a melee attack, P1-marksman-start-4 moved to 7,4 for ranged follow-up, and P1-raider-start-3 moved to 3,3 to capture center-northwest. Raider and marksman each used 1 deck damage to remove P2-raider-start-1. P1 had 10 supply after income, so recruiting P1-raider-turn-005 at 10,1 converted center income into board pressure.\" --strict-win
bun run --silent validate-run -- --strict --strict-deck --strict-win ../runs/codex-rush-vs-engine-01/timeline.json"

item.text:
Completed `turn-005` for P1 and strict validation passed.

P1 played `blast` plus `zap`, bought `storm`, killed `P2-raider-start-1`, captured `center-east` and `center-northwest`, and recruited `P1-raider-turn-005`.
